Definition.text: Gain as Extra is a damage modifier that adds a percentage of a source damage type as another damage type while keeping the original source damage.

Purpose.text: Increase damage coverage by producing additional damage in a target type without removing the original type.

Core Rules.text: Converted-to and Gain as Extra are distinct forms; conversion removes source share, Gain as Extra preserves source share and adds extra.

Internal Rules.text: Within a source damage type, Gain as Extra is resolved before Converted To for that source type.

Calculation Order.text: Conversion order uses the source-type sequence Physical -> Lightning -> Cold -> Fire -> Chaos; Gain as Extra is evaluated in that loop before Converted To in each source bucket.

Priority Rules.text: Specific source wording such as source type scope and radius/amount limits overrides generic assumptions.

Preconditions.text: A hit damage event with one or more damage components and at least one Gain as Extra modifier.

Postconditions.text: Source damage remains part of outgoing damage and extra damage is added as the destination type.

Limitations.text: DoT behavior is distinguished by modifiers that say 'from hits' when applicable.

Caps.text: No standalone cap for Gain as Extra is stated in this source.

Formula.text: For source A and destination B: extra_AB = base_A * g_AB; out_A retains base_A; out_B receives +extra_AB before source A conversion stage proceeds.

cannot_affect: already removed conversion output in same bucket before evaluation

text: Confusion with Converted To when both appear on same or linked modifiers
PoE1 and PoE2 terminology behavior may differ in unrelated patch lines; this entry is for current PoE1 wording as captured in source context.

Hidden Rules.text: Looping through source order allows gain effects to be applied before conversion steps in that bucket.

Developer Notes.text: Keep Gain as Extra and Converted To as separate nodes in schema; do not collapse into one rule.

Historical Changes.text: No explicit historical patch list for this term in this pass.

text.note: Current captured semantics preserve source damage and add destination extra in source order.

text: Gain 20% of Physical Damage as Extra Fire Damage preserves the original physical portion and adds 20% of physical as fire.
If Physical is also converted elsewhere, source-step order still applies Gain as Extra before the conversion step for Physical.
Treating Gain as Extra as converted damage (it is additive).
Applying source modifiers to damage after source is removed (order differs).

prevents: source damage deletion by this node
cannot_affect: already finalized converted share in same step
